https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/commit/.commit_message: [Enhancement] Toggle export auto open from popover (#5946)

description.text: ## Description This PR restyles some of the popovers in the toolbar to increase element spacing. ## Changes The `tags`, `export`, and `pomodoro` popovers were restyled to increase spacing between elements. The export popover had its elements wrapped in a `div` so that the styling was applied only to the popover. The pomodoro start and stop buttons were wrapped in a `div` so they could be centered and widened. ## Additional information # Current # PR --- Tested on: macOS 15.6 and Fedora 42

ranked_scenarios.summary: fix(exporter): Also consider custom commands when checking last profile

closed_issues.summary: Default to Last-Used Export Even When Custom Command

description.text:
### Description

When using a defaults profile to export a document, Zettlr 3.1.1 (on Windows 11) defaults to that profile in the next export. When using a custom command, however, Zettlr defaults to a blank export. If this blank export is run (say, because the user inattentively doesn't select something else), Zettlr replaces the existing markdown file with a new markdown file with many (or all) of the markdown characters escaped. Defaulting to the same custom command would both provide an added convenience and avoid potentially overwriting an existing markdown file with a new file where the markdown is "broken."

![Screenshot 2024-05-15 074053](https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/assets/205528/fd6fe685-6d4a-40f1-97fa-2635785c7956)
![Screenshot 2024-05-15 074133](https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/assets/205528/96921778-cc0f-4102-9ff6-e13bfb219a59)
![Screenshot 2024-05-15 074145](https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/assets/205528/29b9bbbf-f6b1-4ad8-bc3d-8936cffc4da7)

### Proposed Changes

The behavior to default to custom export commands should be the same as the behavior of currently defaulting to exports specified via defaults files.

### Caveats

I don't believe there are any, and there's the additional negative outcome of overwriting an existing markdown file that's avoided. Of course, if one wants to overwrite an existing file, one can create an export to do just that separately.

### Do you Wish to Attempt Implementing this Yourself?

No

### Zettlr Version

Stable (most recent version)

### Your Platform

- [X] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Operating System Version

Windows 10 and 11

### Additional Information

The existing UI would work great as shown in the screenshots above---provided that Zettlr remembers and defaults to the most recently run export (whether that's based on a defaults file or a custom command).

## Description

This PR adds the ability to add custom commands to Zettlr. This lets users define certain commands that only receive the file or project folder path to export and can then handle a lot by themselves.

## Changes

Added a new configuration setting that allows users to add custom commands to Zettlr and giving them a proper display name. These commands can subsequently be selected in the single file export popover as well as the project settings and enable users to export their files and projects arbitrarily. This could, for example, be a shell script, a docker container, or anything really that processes them and does something to them.

**PLEASE NOTE** that in order to facilitate the new setting in the preferences, I had to heavily rework the ListControl as well. It is now fully typed, has more sanity checks and better support for, e.g., proper column labels. I took the liberty to type the entire preferences form control as well, so you may ignore those changes while reviewing.

## Additional information

Tested on: macOS Ventura 13.4.1. (c)

Closes #1842

## Description
An enhancement would be if one could execute arbitrary commands in a custom export function. Thus, it would be possible to use the editor's Markdown e.g. with a Docker command and produce all kinds of output with Zettlr.

## Proposed Changes
- Add a new icon to the list of export formats named something like "custom"
- Add a new section to the setting tab "Advanced" that relates to this icon.
- Add a new form field to this section that takes custom commands being executed on the text corpus when the custom icon is clicked.

## Caveats
Not imaginable yet. But could be that the custom pandoc command is no longer necessary with this enhancement.

## Additional Information
Main reason for this enhancement request is that we would like to use Zettlr more in the context of the single-source production we have developed in https://doi.org/10.15480/882.2902
